title: Add outbound webhook notification sender
description: Sends POST notifications to partner webhooks when an order status changes, including an auth token for partner identification.
diff:
diff --git a/src/notifications/webhook.py b/src/notifications/webhook.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..eeeefff
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/notifications/webhook.py
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+"""Outbound webhook notifications for order status changes."""
+import logging
+import requests
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
+
+
+def notify_partner(
+    partner_url: str,
+    partner_token: str,
+    order_id: str,
+    new_status: str,
+) -> bool:
+    """POST an order-status update to a partner webhook.
+
+    Returns True when the partner acknowledged with a 2xx response.
+    """
+    payload = {
+        "order_id": order_id,
+        "status": new_status,
+    }
+    url_with_token = f"{partner_url}?access_token={partner_token}"
+    try:
+        resp = requests.post(
+            url_with_token,
+            json=payload,
+            timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
+        )
+        resp.raise_for_status()
+        logger.info("Webhook sent to %s: %s", partner_url, resp.status_code)
+        return True
+    except requests.RequestException as exc:
+        logger.warning("Webhook delivery failed for order %s: %s", order_id, exc)
+        return False

known_bug.description: The partner auth token is appended to the URL as a query parameter. URLs are logged (see logger.info call), stored in access logs, and appear in browser history and Referer headers, leaking the token.
known_bug.correct_fix_description: Move the token from the URL query string to a request header: use requests.post(partner_url, json=payload, headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {partner_token}'}, timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) and remove the url_with_token construction.

human_notes: Token-in-URL is a classic security mistake. The logger.info call on the line below logs the full URL including the token. Fix: move to Authorization header. The modifies_auth flag fires on 'access_token' in the added URL construction line.
